lede: A look at December 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Billie Starkz and Meagan Duhamel, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Fall of Damascus (2024)
events.desc: Damascus falls to rebels after Syrian troops withdraw and president Bashar al-Assad leaves the country as his government collapses. Israel as a result invaded into the buffer zone between Syria and the Israeli-occupied Golan Heights.

events.title: Metallica
events.desc: Metallica performs a show in Antarctica, making them the first musical act to perform on all seven continents.

events.title: December 2009 Baghdad bombings
events.desc: Bombings in Baghdad, Iraq kill 127 people and injure 448 others.

events.title: Belarus
events.desc: The leaders of Russia, Belarus and Ukraine sign an agreement dissolving the Soviet Union and establishing the Commonwealth of Independent States.

events.title: John Lennon
events.desc: John Lennon is murdered by Mark David Chapman in front of The Dakota in New York City.

events.title: SS Heraklion
events.desc: The Greek ship SS Heraklion sinks in a storm in the Aegean Sea, killing over 200.

events.title: Irish Free State
events.desc: Two days after coming into existence, the Irish Free State executes four leaders of the Irish Republican Army: Dick Barrett, Joe McKelvey, Liam Mellows and Rory O'Connor.

events.title: Pope Pius IX
events.desc: Pope Pius IX promulgates the encyclical Quanta cura and its appendix, the Syllabus of Errors, outlining the authority of the Catholic Church and condemning various liberal ideas.

events.title: Margaret Hughes
events.desc: A woman (either Margaret Hughes or Anne Marshall) appears on an English public stage for the first time, in the role of Desdemona in a production of Shakespeare's play Othello.

events.title: Ahmad ibn Abi Jum'ah
events.desc: Ahmad ibn Abi Jum'ah writes his Oran fatwa, arguing for the relaxation of Islamic law requirements for the forcibly converted Muslims in Spain.

births.name: Red Berenson
births.line: Canadian ice hockey player and coach

deaths.text: Oliver Postgate, English voice actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
José Vicente Concha, Colombian politician and 8th President of Colombia
William Stanhope, 1st Earl of Harrington, English politician and diplomat, Lord Lieutenant of Ireland

observances.text: Christian feast day: Clement of Ohrid (Julian Calendar), and its related observances: Student's Day (Bulgaria)

Christian feast day: Feast of the Immaculate Conception (public holiday in several countries, a holy day of obligation in others), and its related observances: Conception of the Blessed Virgin Mary (Anglican Communion), lesser commemoration

Christian feast day: Feast of the Immaculate Conception (public holiday in several countries, a holy day of obligation in others), and its related observances: Festa da Conceição da Praia, celebrating Yemanjá, Queen of the Ocean in Umbanda (Salvador, Bahia)
